REQUEST FOR CONFIRMATTON OF ADVICE

ivlr. john lVallacc
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
P. O. Box 807
Sacramento, California 95804
Dear Mr. Wallace:
Pursuant to our telephone conversation ofJuly 7,1992,I am hereby requesting written
confirmation of your advice regarding the elected ofEcials of the City otTurlock as follows:
Re:
Conflict of Interest - Mayor and Council Member
Dr. Curt Andre - Background. Elected Mayor in April, 1990. Dr. Andre is a local,
practicing optometrist within the City of Turlock and one-hundred
percent (l00%o) shareholder in Andre, Inc. He conducts this practice
with Dr. Jack M. Makin, both receiving a salary from the corporation.
On June 9 and 23, 1992, a patient (Mr. & Mrs. Kiousis) of Dr. Andre's
had a matter before the City Council wherein the mayor stepped down
pursuant to California Govcrnment Code $87103(c), to a'.'oiC n conflict.
Facts. The facts of that conflict are as follows: In June of 1991, the
Kiousis's adult daughter received services in the amount o[ One
Hundred Eighty-eight Dollars ($188), inJuly o[ 1991, Kiousis's
dependent son received service of Sixty Dollars ($601 and in April of
1992 Mrs. Kiousis received services of one Hundred Fifty (glb0) from
Dr. Andre. The total paylent to Dr. Andre within the preceding
twelve months was, rherefore, Three Hundred Ninety-eight Dollirs
($?98I On June 23, 1992 at the second reading, the'Cit! Council
(with 3 council members sitting) denied adoption of the Kiousis,s
prezone ordinance, without prejudice, by a 5-0 vote. Mr. & Mrs.
Kiousis have refiled their application wtrich will first go before the
Planning commission and will come before the city Louncil in
December 1992 orJanuary lgg3.
post office Box T . Turrock, carifornia g53g1_1526




Mr. Johq Wallace
Fair Political Practices Commission
Jr/y 6, 1992
Pnge 2
Ron Hillberg
Inquiry. Pursuant to Government Code $87103(c) and based upon
the [acts reflected herein, is it a disqualifying conflict For Dr. Andre to
vote on this matter when it returns to the Council in December or
January? As reflected from the foregoing facts, Dr. Andre will have
received One Hundred Fifty Dollars ($150) in income [rom Mr. & Mrs.
Kiousis "within the l2 months prior to the time when the decision is
made."
Advice. You advised that based on the facts as stated, there would be
no conflict or disqualification o[the mayor. In other words, a conflict
of interest does not exist ifa new application is submitted resulting in
the process beginning again and when the income received from Mr.
& Mrs. Kiousis within twelve months prior to the date the decision is
made would be less than Two Hundred Filty Dollars ($250).
I also ask what the answer would have been if the City Council had on
June 23rd, granted the Kiousis's request to have the June 9th matter
continued to the August 25th meeting. On this you advised that the
amount received by Dr. Andre would be the same as reflected above,
i.e., he would have received only One Hundred Fifty Dollars ($150) lor
his April services to the Kiousis family-resulting, accordingly, in the
finding o[no conflict. However, you advised that a continuance o[the
June 9th matter would have been a "grayer" area than what actually
occurred which was that the Council denied adoption and, therefore,
Mr. & Mrs. Kiousis will be coming before the Council on an entirely
new application for a prezone.
Background. Elected Council Member in April, 1988. Mr. Hillberg
is an a'"torney practicing law rvithin the City o[Turlock. On Junc I
and 23, 1992, Mr. Hillberg also stepped down pursuant ro Government
Code $87100 et seq. to avoid a conflict o[ interest. Specifically, Mr.
Hillberg announced that he had a client (not the perion beflore the
Council) who owned property in the vicinity of the Kiousis prezone.
Mr. Hillberg stepped down believing that he was required to do so to
avoid a conflict of interest. Mr. Hillberg stated that the prezone
ordinance might have a financial impact on his client's property.
Inquiry. Is it a disqualifring conflict for Mr. Hillberg to vote on a
matter which comes before the City Council regarding property which
is not his client's property but may, floreseeably, have a financial
impact on his client's property?




Mr. John Wallace
Fair Political Practices Commission
July 6, 1992
Pege 3
Advice. You advised that based on the facts as stated above, Mr.
Hillberg was disqualified due to a conflict of interest. This situation
must be approached as though the client's property belonged to Mr.
Hillberg, is within 300'olthe prezone property and therefore has the
potential of increasing in value. Mr. Hillberg is disqualified flrom
participating in this matter due to such potential financial benefit to
the property by the City Council's action.
I appreciate your kind assistance in this matter. Should you require any more information,
please leel free to contact me.
Thank you for your courtesy and cooperation in this matter.
APM:lk
P. MORRIS, City Attorney

sections.question: Pursuant to Government Code §87103(c) and based upon the facts reflected herein, is it a disqualifying conflict for Dr. Andre to vote on this matter when it returns to the Council in December or January? As reflected from the foregoing facts, Dr. Andre will have received One Hundred Fifty Dollars ($150) in income from Mr. & Mrs. Kiousis "within the 12 months prior to the time when the decision is made." Additionally, is it a disqualifying conflict for Mr. Hillberg to vote on a matter which comes before the City Council regarding property which is not his client's property but may, foreseeably, have a financial impact on his client's property?
sections.conclusion: Regarding Dr. Andre: Based on the facts as stated, there would be no conflict or disqualification of the mayor. A conflict of interest does not exist if a new application is submitted resulting in the process beginning again and when the income received from Mr. & Mrs. Kiousis within twelve months prior to the date the decision is made would be less than Two Hundred Fifty Dollars ($250). If the Council had granted a continuance to August 25th, this would have been a "grayer" area, but since the Council denied adoption and the Kiousis's will be coming before the Council on an entirely new application, no conflict exists. Regarding Mr. Hillberg: Based on the facts as stated, Mr. Hillberg is disqualified due to a conflict of interest. This situation must be approached as though the client's property belonged to Mr. Hillberg, is within 300' of the prezone property and therefore has the potential of increasing in value. Mr. Hillberg is disqualified from participating in this matter due to such potential financial benefit to the property by the City Council's action.

Hillberg, is within 300'olthe prezone property and therefore has the
potential of increasing in value. Mr. Hillberg is disqualified flrom

the property by the City Council's action.
I appreciate your kind assistance in this matter. Should you require any more information,
please leel free to contact me.
Thank you for your courtesy and cooperation in this matter.

P. MORRIS, City Attorney
sections.question_synthetic: The City Attorney requests written confirmation of FPPC advice regarding whether Mayor Dr. Andre has a disqualifying conflict of interest under Government Code §87103(c) when voting on the Kiousis matter if it returns to Council, and whether Council Member Hillberg is disqualified from voting on matters affecting property in the vicinity of his client's property.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: FPPC advised that Mayor Dr. Andre has no disqualifying conflict of interest if the Kiousis matter returns as a new application with less than $250 in income received within twelve months, but a continuance would present a grayer area. FPPC also advised that Council Member Hillberg is disqualified from voting due to potential financial benefit to his client's nearby property.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: old; LLM: This is an incoming request letter from city attorney seeking confirmation of oral advice previously given. Document includes both the questions asked and the advice allegedly given verbatim. Some OCR errors present in original (e.g., 'Tirloch' for Turlock, 'calibrnia' for California). Formatted as formal letter requesting written confirmation rather than as structured advice letter.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Pursuant to Government Code §87103(c) and based upon the facts reflected herein, is it a disqualifying conflict for Dr. Andre to vote on this matter when it returns to the Council in December or January? As reflected from the foregoing facts, Dr. Andre will have received One Hundred Fifty Dollars ($150) in income from Mr. & Mrs. Kiousis "within the 12 months prior to the time when the decision is made." Additionally, is it a disqualifying conflict for Mr. Hillberg to vote on a matter which comes before the City Council regarding property which is not his client's property but may, foreseeably, have a financial impact on his client's property?

CONCLUSION: Regarding Dr. Andre: Based on the facts as stated, there would be no conflict or disqualification of the mayor. A conflict of interest does not exist if a new application is submitted resulting in the process beginning again and when the income received from Mr. & Mrs. Kiousis within twelve months prior to the date the decision is made would be less than Two Hundred Fifty Dollars ($250). If the Council had granted a continuance to August 25th, this would have been a "grayer" area, but since the Council denied adoption and the Kiousis's will be coming before the Council on an entirely new application, no conflict exists. Regarding Mr. Hillberg: Based on the facts as stated, Mr. Hillberg is disqualified due to a conflict of interest. This situation must be approached as though the client's property belonged to Mr. Hillberg, is within 300' of the prezone property and therefore has the potential of increasing in value. Mr. Hillberg is disqualified from participating in this matter due to such potential financial benefit to the property by the City Council's action.

embedding.summary: The City Attorney of Turlock requests written confirmation of FPPC telephone advice regarding conflict of interest determinations for Mayor Dr. Andre and Council Member Ron Hillberg concerning a prezone ordinance matter involving the Kiousis family. The document records FPPC's verbal advice on both officials' disqualifications under Government Code conflict of interest provisions.
